commit.message:
feat: Editor text decorations, language server support, and IDE demo (#220)

* feat: Add text decoration API for editor syntax highlighting

Implement Phase 1 of language server support — a low-level text decoration
API that enables per-character styling (foreground, background, bold, italic)
in the editor widget.

New types:
- TextDecoration: describes visual styling with theme-aware color resolution
- TextDecorationSpan: maps document position ranges to decorations with priority
- ITextDecorationProvider: interface for supplying decorations per render frame
- IEditorSession: handle for providers to interact with the editor
- SyntaxTheme: theme elements for common semantic token types (keyword, string,
  comment, number, type, function, etc.)

Editor integration:
- EditorWidget.Decorations(provider) fluent API for attaching providers
- TextEditorViewRenderer collects decorations from providers, resolves per-line
  decorations via BuildLineDecorations(), and applies them in RenderLine()
- Cursor/selection styling takes priority over decorations
- Multiple overlapping decorations resolved by priority per attribute

Demo & tests:
- LanguageServerDemo sample with TabPanel and C# syntax highlighting tab
- CSharpSyntaxHighlighter: static syntax highlighter using ITextDecorationProvider
- 8 tests covering fg/bg colors, cursor override, priority resolution,
  theme elements, multi-line spans, and combined attributes

Key fix: ResolvedDecoration array must be initialized with Hex1bColor.Default
(not default(Hex1bColor) which is RGB(0,0,0) with IsDefault=false).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add underline decoration rendering with capability fallback

- Add UnderlineStyle and UnderlineColor to ResolvedDecoration struct
- Update BuildLineDecorations to resolve underline styles with priority
- Update RenderLine to emit underline ANSI codes (SGR 4:x, SGR 58)
- Implement fallback: curly/dotted/dashed → single when terminal lacks
  styled underline support; skip underline color when unsupported
- Add UnderlineColorThemeElement to TextDecoration for theme-aware
  underline colors
- Add DiagnosticTheme with error/warning/info/hint underline colors
- Add SupportsStyledUnderlines and SupportsUnderlineColor to
  TerminalCapabilities
- Create DiagnosticHighlighter demo provider with pattern-based
  error/warning/info/hint underlines
- Add Diagnostics tab to LanguageServerDemo sample
- Write 7 underline tests covering all styles, fallback, and theme
  resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix underline rendering, color shift bug, and add line numbers

- Fix ConsolePresentationAdapter missing SupportsStyledUnderlines and
  SupportsUnderlineColor capabilities, which caused underlines to not
  render in real terminal sessions
- Rewrite RenderLine decoration state management to track active
  decoration state explicitly, properly resetting SGR attributes before
  cell type transitions (Normal→Cursor, Cursor→Normal) to prevent
  color shifts when cursoring through decorated text
- Add LineNumbers() fluent API to EditorWidget with line number gutter
  rendering in EditorNode, including gutter width calculation, viewport
  adjustment, and HitTest offset correction
- Add EditorTheme.LineNumberForegroundColor theme support
- Enable line numbers in LanguageServerDemo for both tabs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Add underline style and color support to Surface rendering pipeline

The Surface pipeline (SurfaceCell → SurfaceRenderContext → SurfaceComparer)
was stripping underline data — the ANSI sequences emitted by RenderLine
were parsed into cells that had no underline fields, causing all underline
information to be lost before reaching the terminal.

- Add UnderlineStyle and UnderlineColor fields to SurfaceCell
- Add colon sub-parameter parsing to SurfaceRenderContext.ParseSgrSequence
  for SGR 4:x (curly/dotted/dashed underlines)
- Add SGR 58 (underline color) parsing in both semicolon and colon syntax
- Add SGR 21 (double underline) and SGR 59 (default underline color) support
- Pass underline state when creating SurfaceCell instances
- Update SurfaceComparer.BuildSgrParameters to emit styled underline codes
  (4:3, 4:4, 4:5) and underline color (58;2;R;G;B) in output
- Track underline style/color state in the comparer diff loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: Enable mouse support in LanguageServerDemo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add editor overlay system with push/dismiss and hover demo

- EditorOverlay, OverlayLine, OverlayPlacement types for anchored UI
- IEditorSession expanded with PushOverlay/DismissOverlay/ActiveOverlays
- EditorNode implements IEditorSession with overlay lifecycle management
- Activate/Deactivate lifecycle for ITextDecorationProvider
- Overlay rendering with box-drawing borders and coordinate mapping
- DismissOnCursorMove support for hover-style overlays
- HoverInfoProvider demo showing diagnostic hover on cursor position
- Hover Info tab in LanguageServerDemo
- 9 overlay tests covering push/dismiss/lifecycle/rendering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Language Server Protocol integration

Phase 4 implementation — complete LSP client with in-process test server:

Protocol layer:
- JsonRpcTransport: Content-Length framed JSON-RPC 2.0 over streams
  with concurrent read dispatching (request TCS + notification events)
- LspTypes: Position, Range, SemanticTokens, Diagnostics, Completion
- LspMessages: JSON-RPC request/response/notification types

Client:
- LanguageServerClient: lifecycle management (stdio/socket/transport),
  initialize handshake, document sync, semantic tokens, completions
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider: ITextDecorationProvider that bridges
  LSP semantic tokens + diagnostics to decoration spans
- SemanticTokenMapper: delta-encoded LSP tokens → TextDecorationSpans
- DiagnosticMapper: LSP diagnostics → underline decorations by severity

Public API:
- .LanguageServer(config => ...) extension on EditorWidget
- .WithServerCommand(), .WithSocket(), .WithTransport() for connections
- .WithLanguageId(), .EnableSemanticHighlighting(), .EnableDiagnostics()

Testing:
- TestLanguageServer: in-process LSP server over System.IO.Pipelines
- 7 integration tests: init, semantic tokens, diagnostics, completions
- Tests verify full pipeline: server → transport → client → mappers

Demo:
- InProcessLanguageServer for the LanguageServerDemo sample
- 'Language Server' tab showing LSP-powered syntax + diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix LSP highlighting not appearing in demo

Three root causes:
1. LanguageServer() extension created a new provider every render frame,
   killing the LSP connection before it could return tokens. Fixed by
   caching providers via ConditionalWeakTable keyed by delegate identity.
2. IEditorSession.Invalidate() was a no-op — async token responses never
   triggered re-render. Now calls MarkDirty().
3. Demo used .LanguageServer() inline. Changed to pre-create provider
   outside the builder for stable instance identity.

Also made LanguageServerDecorationProvider public so consumers can create
and manage provider lifecycle explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace for shared server connections

LSP servers bind to a workspace root and handle multiple documents over
a single connection. The new workspace concept models this:

- Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace: manages one LanguageServerClient per
  registered language, shared across all documents of that language
- RegisterServer(languageId, config): registers server configuration
  (lazy — server starts when first document opens)
- GetProvider(documentUri, languageId?): returns a cached decoration
  provider for a document, backed by the shared server connection
- Language ID inference from file extension (.cs→csharp, .py→python, etc.)

API usage:
  var workspace = new Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace("/my/project");
  workspace.RegisterServer("csharp", lsp => lsp.WithServerCommand("csharp-ls"));
  ctx.Editor(state).LanguageServer(workspace, "file:///my/project/File.cs")

Also refactored:
- LanguageServerClient: multi-document (URI per method, version tracking
  per document, close all on shutdown)
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider: supports shared client via internal
  constructor, filters diagnostics by document URI
- Demo: Language Server tab shows two editors sharing one server

4 workspace tests + 7 LSP integration tests, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Hex1bDocumentWorkspace with file persistence and server mapping

- Hex1bDocument: FilePath, IsDirty, SaveAsync for file-backed documents
- Hex1bDocumentWorkspace: OpenDocumentAsync, CreateDocument, SaveAllAsync
- AddLanguageServer/MapLanguageServer for glob-based server resolution
- GetProvider resolves server automatically from document file extension
- .LanguageServer(workspace) extension on EditorWidget
- 15 new tests for document management, dirty tracking, and server mapping
- Demo: Doc Workspace tab showing new API with two editors sharing a server

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix LSP highlighting race condition with client readiness

Both workspace classes (Hex1bLanguageServerWorkspace, Hex1bDocumentWorkspace)
start the LanguageServerClient in a fire-and-forget Task.Run(). The provider
would then try to call OpenDocumentAsync() before StartAsync() completed,
hitting _transport == null and silently failing.

Fix:
- Add TaskCompletionSource-based WaitUntilReadyAsync() to LanguageServerClient
- Provider's OpenAndRefreshAsync() and OnDocumentChangedAsync() now await
  client readiness before sending any requests
- Extract token legend after readiness (workspace may not have had time
  to populate the legend when the provider was first created)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Real language server demo with csharp-ls and typescript-language-server

Replace mock syntax highlighting with real LSP servers:
- csharp-ls (Roslyn-based, dotnet tool) for C# semantic tokens + diagnostics
- typescript-language-server (tsserver-backed) for TypeScript highlighting

Demo workspace with real files:
- workspace/Greeter.cs — C# classes with intentional diagnostic patterns
- workspace/TaskManager.ts — TypeScript task manager with interfaces/generics
- workspace/Demo.csproj + tsconfig.json — project files for LSP analysis

Infrastructure changes:
- LanguageServerConfiguration.WorkingDirectory for server process CWD
- LanguageServerClient sets ProcessStartInfo.WorkingDirectory
- Hex1bDocumentWorkspace passes root path as WorkingDirectory

Improved CSharpSyntaxHighlighter (static fallback):
- Block comment tracking across lines
- Verbatim/interpolated/character string literals
- Preprocessor directives, hex/binary number literals
- Context-aware type detection (after class/struct, IFoo, Foo<T>)
- Method call detection (word followed by parenthesis)
- Removed broken PascalCase-is-type heuristic

Improved InProcessLanguageServer (in-process fallback):
- Same context-aware token generation as the static highlighter
- Block comments, verbatim strings, method/type detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Switch to typescript-language-server and fix LSP transport

- Replace broken csharp-ls integration with working typescript-language-server
  (csharp-ls v0.22.0 has a bug where it never loads solutions in LSP mode)
- Add server-to-client request handling in JsonRpcTransport (auto-responds to
  requests like client/registerCapability that servers send)
- Add WorkspaceFolders to InitializeParams for proper LSP initialization
- Catch all exceptions in notification loop to prevent silent crashes
- Add workspace .sln file for potential future C# LS support
- Exclude workspace .cs files from demo compilation
- Real language server integration tests pass (3s, 126+ tokens)
- 5597 tests pass, 28 skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add LSP completion popup with interactive selection

- CompletionController manages popup state: show, navigate, accept, dismiss
- EditorNode wires completion triggers: '.' and Ctrl+Space invoke LSP
- Up/Down navigate items, Enter accepts, Escape dismisses
- Overlay renders with kind icons, selection highlighting, scroll support
- CompletionItem.InsertText preferred over Label when accepting
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider exposes client/URI for completion access
- Added CompletionItemKind constants (Enum through TypeParameter)
- 10 CompletionController unit tests + 1 real LS integration test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Add CompletionContext and client-side prefix filtering

- CompletionParams now includes CompletionContext (triggerKind + triggerCharacter)
- '.' trigger sends triggerKind=2 (TriggerCharacter), Ctrl+Space sends triggerKind=1 (Invoked)
- CompletionController.Filter() narrows items by prefix as user types
- Accept() subtracts already-typed prefix from inserted text
- Case-insensitive matching, uses FilterText when available on items
- Added filterText/sortText fields to CompletionItem
- 5 new filtering tests (15 total CompletionController tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Rebuild LanguageServerDemo as full IDE experience

- Creates temp workspace at startup with package.json, tsconfig.json,
  and sample TS/JS/C# files for proper LSP scoping
- File Explorer tree view with categorized files (TypeScript, JavaScript,
  C#, Config) using Hex1bDocumentWorkspace
- Tabbed editor with preview/keep-open semantics (click vs double-click)
- MenuBar with File (Save/Close/Exit), Edit, View, Help menus
- InfoBar showing cursor position, language, dirty state, tab count
- LSP-powered highlighting for .ts/.js files via workspace mapping
- Static C# highlighting via CSharpSyntaxHighlighter
- Dirty indicator (●) on modified tab titles
- Temp workspace cleaned up on exit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Restructure demo as real TypeScript project

Temp workspace now mirrors a realistic TypeScript project:
- package.json with name, scripts, devDependencies
- tsconfig.json with proper moduleResolution, rootDir, outDir
- src/models/ — Task, User, AuthToken domain types with exports
- src/services/ — TaskService with event emitter, AuthService
- src/utils/ — helpers (generateId, matchesFilter, timeAgo), EventEmitter
- src/index.ts — entry point importing and using all modules
- Files import each other with proper relative paths

File explorer tree now reflects real directory structure (src/models/,
src/services/, src/utils/) instead of flat category groups. Removed
C# files and static highlighter — all files use LSP via workspace.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Sync document before completions + add embedded terminal

Completion fix:
- Send didChange to LS before requesting completions so the server
  has the latest document content (including the trigger character).
  Without this, 'auth.' completions returned all global symbols
  because the LS still had the pre-edit content.

Demo enhancement:
- Added embedded bash terminal in a VSplitter below the editor area,
  launched in the scope of the temp TypeScript workspace directory.
- Terminal panel has header label and separator, fills remaining space.
- Proper cleanup: cancel terminal CTS and dispose on exit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add diagnostics panel with Problems tab in bottom panel

Library changes:
- Add DiagnosticInfo record (public API) with severity, message,
  position, file name, and display helpers (icon, label)
- Add DiagnosticMapper.MapToDiagnosticInfo() to convert LSP diagnostics
  to public DiagnosticInfo records
- Expose CurrentDiagnostics on LanguageServerDecorationProvider
- Add GetAllDiagnostics() and GetDiagnosticSummary() to
  Hex1bDocumentWorkspace for cross-document aggregation

Demo changes:
- Replace plain terminal bottom panel with a TabPanel containing
  'Terminal' and 'Problems' tabs
- Problems tab shows a Table with severity icon, message, file, line/col
- Activating a row navigates to the file containing the diagnostic
- Info bar shows error/warning counts from workspace diagnostics
- View menu items to switch between Terminal and Problems tabs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Make diagnostics table fill space and navigate cursor on Enter

- Table uses .Fill() instead of .FillHeight() to fill the panel
- Added .Focus() + .OnFocusChanged() for keyboard navigation
- OnRowActivated now opens the file AND sets the editor cursor to the
  exact diagnostic position via SetCursorPosition + PositionToOffset
- Status bar shows the diagnostic message when navigating

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Transfer focus to editor when activating a diagnostic row

Use displayApp.RequestFocus(node => node is EditorNode) after
opening the file and setting the cursor position, so double-clicking
a problem in the table moves focus directly into the editor at the
diagnostic location.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Fix diagnostic navigation to correctly resolve files and focus

- Match diagnostics to workspace files by relative path extracted from
  the document URI instead of fragile filename EndsWith matching
- Add Invalidate() after RequestFocus() to trigger the render cycle
  that processes the pending focus predicate
- Wrap PositionToOffset in try/catch for robustness
- Set cursor position even if the file was already open (not just when
  newly opened via OpenFile)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Implement Enter key activation for table rows

TableNode.OnRowActivated was a stub — the handler was stored but never
invoked. Added:
- Enter key binding in ConfigureDefaultBindings that calls
  ActivateFocusedRow
- ActivateFocusedRow method that finds the focused row by key and
  invokes the RowActivatedHandler

This enables the diagnostics table (and any table with OnRowActivated)
to respond to Enter/double-click to navigate to the relevant code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add double-click activation for table rows

Added a MouseButton.Left.DoubleClick() binding in TableNode that
resolves the clicked row by mouse position and invokes
RowActivatedHandler. This complements the Enter key activation
added in the previous commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add in-process git diff syntax highlighter

New GitDiffDecorationProvider — an ITextDecorationProvider that runs
entirely in-process (no external language server). Highlights unified
diff syntax: diff/index headers, ---/+++ file paths, @@ hunk headers,
+/- addition/deletion lines with colored foreground and tinted
backgrounds.

Workspace API for in-process providers:
- AddDecorationProvider(id, factory) registers a provider factory
- MapDecorationProvider(glob, id) maps file patterns to providers
- GetDecorationProvider() checks in-process mappings first, then
  falls back to language server mappings
- LanguageServer() extension now uses GetDecorationProvider() so
  all file types get decorations through a single call

Demo updates:
- Added recent-changes.diff sample file to workspace
- All file types now go through workspace decoration resolution
- Added .diff/.patch icon and language label in info bar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add soft line wrapping for text editor

Add WordWrap property to EditorWidget with fluent API and reconcile
it through to EditorNode. Implement wrapping in TextEditorViewRenderer
with a WrapLine helper that breaks at word boundaries (falling back to
hard breaks) and a RenderWrapped method that maps wrapped display lines
back to document positions for cursor/selection/decoration rendering.

- Add wordWrap parameter to IEditorViewRenderer.Render() (default false)
- Suppress horizontal scrollbar in EditorNode when WordWrap is enabled
- Create LineWrapTheme for wrap indicator styling
- Update HexEditorViewRenderer to accept and ignore the parameter
- Add 14 unit tests covering WrapLine, widget API, and reconciliation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fold+scroll lockup, LSP re-render, and single-line fold filtering

- Add AppInvalidate callback on Hex1bNode base class so nodes can wake
  the render loop for async updates (LSP notifications). Set by Hex1bApp
  during reconciliation. EditorNode.Invalidate() now calls both
  MarkDirty() and AppInvalidate, ensuring diagnostics appear immediately.

- Filter out single-line folding regions in FoldingRangesToRegions()
  where endLine <= startLine after 0->1-based conversion, preventing
  useless fold indicators in the gutter.

- Clamp _horizontalScrollOffset and _scrollOffset in ArrangeCore after
  viewport dimension changes from folding or scrollbar toggling. Reset
  horizontal scroll when scrollbar is hidden.

- Make scrollbar click handlers only call MarkDirty() when offset
  actually changes, preventing unnecessary re-render storms.

- Robustify CalculateHorizontalThumb and CalculateVerticalThumb against
  division by zero and scroll fractions outside [0,1].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add README.md with feature guide to LanguageServerDemo workspace

Insert a README.md into the temporary workspace created by the
LanguageServerDemo sample. The file documents all editor hotkeys,
LSP features (completions, hover, go-to-definition, references,
folding), automatic features (semantic tokens, diagnostics, inlay
hints, breadcrumbs), IDE panels, and usage tips.

Also add a 📖 icon mapping for .md files in the file tree builder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Ctrl+Space not reaching editor completion handler

Ctrl+Space sends a NUL byte (0x00) in traditional terminals, but
ParseKeyInput in both Hex1bTerminal and Hex1bAppWorkloadAdapter
did not handle the NUL case — it fell through to null and was
silently dropped.

Add '\0' → Hex1bKey.Spacebar with Hex1bModifiers.Control in both
ParseKeyInput implementations.

Also add CSI u (fixterm/Kitty protocol) support to ParseCsiSequence
so that modern terminals sending ESC[32;5u for Ctrl+Space are
handled correctly. The new ParseFixtermKeycode method maps Unicode
codepoints to Hex1bKey values for common keys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix completion filtering and prevent double didChange sync

- CompletionController.Filter: Add Contains fallback alongside StartsWith
  for broader matching (e.g., typing 'Coll' matches 'groupCollapsed')
- EditorNode.InsertTextAsync: Accept underscore in filter prefix, not just
  letters and digits
- EditorNode.DeleteBackwardAsync: Add Backspace awareness during active
  completion to shrink filter prefix and re-filter
- LanguageServerDecorationProvider.SyncDocumentAsync: New method that syncs
  document content and updates _lastDocVersion, preventing GetDecorations()
  background sync from double-sending didChange to the language server
- EditorNode.RequestCompletionAtCursorAsync: Use provider.SyncDocumentAsync
  instead of client.ChangeDocumentAsync to eliminate race condition between
  completion request and background document sync

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move UI types to proper namespaces

- Move EditorOverlay, SignaturePanel, ActionMenu from Hex1b.Documents to Hex1b.Widgets
- Move IGutterProvider, DecorationGutterProvider, FoldingGutterProvider, LineNumberGutterProvider from Hex1b to Hex1b.Documents
- Remove redundant self-referencing using statements
- Update all affected consumers with correct using directives

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-derive unique alias for cross-package repo name collisions

When auto-derived alias collides with an existing dep from a different
package (e.g. anthropics/skills vs microsoft/skills), automatically use
org-repo format (microsoft-skills) instead of prompting to replace.

- Distinguish same-package updates from cross-package collisions via
  PackageIdentity() comparison
- Track explicit vs auto-derived aliases; error on explicit collisions
  instead of silently renaming
- Error on unparseable existing dep URLs instead of assuming different package
- Preserve 'Update?' prompt only for same-package version changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add benchmarks directory with scenario-based benchmark suite and fast ChannelIterator (#172)

* Initial plan

* Add benchmarks directory with full benchmark suite

- Framework: BenchConfig (target-tier iteration counts), BenchRunner
  (warmup + measure), BenchResult typedef, import.hx, Macro.hx
- Cases (cases/ subdirectory): BenchBasic, BenchTask, BenchChannel,
  BenchMutex, BenchGenerator
- BenchTask covers 7 task-architecture aspects: sequential_await,
  concurrent_latch, lazy_await, scope, supervisor, timeout_no_expire,
  nested (2-level hierarchy)
- Main.hx: formatted table output, per-target JSON persistence in
  results/latest-<target>.json (gitignored), regression comparison on
  re-run with 10% threshold
- Build files for eval, js, cpp, jvm, hl, python, neko, php
- .gitignore: benchmarks/bin/ and benchmarks/results/

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace micro-benchmarks with scenario-based benchmarks

Per feedback: the previous micro-benchmarks were too granular and
susceptible to GC/JIT jitter (notably on JVM). Replace them with five
application-style scenarios that each run for 0.2–1.5 s, providing a
much higher signal-to-noise ratio.

New cases (benchmarks/src/cases/):
- BenchWorkerPool  – WORKERS coroutines drain a bounded channel
- BenchPipeline    – 3-stage Source → Transform → Sink pipeline
- BenchContendedMutex – WORKERS compete for a mutex; yield() while
  holding forces genuine suspend/resume contention on every acquire
- BenchCancellation – repeated waves of spawning+cancelChildren() in a
  supervisor scope, measuring full cancellation teardown throughput
- BenchFanOut – scatter/gather: spawn WORKERS concurrent tasks (each
  does yield() + computation), then await and aggregate results

BenchGenerator kept unchanged (SelfDispatcher subsystem, already stable).

BenchConfig: replace SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE with WORKERS/MESSAGES/ROUNDS/
GENERATOR_ITERS; 4-tier structure (cpp/jvm/hl, js, python/php, others).

BenchRunner: 5 warmup rounds for JVM (was 1) to reach JIT steady state.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* introduce a performance regression

* Merge result and comparison tables into one

Remove the Iter column and the separate comparison block. printTable()
now accepts an optional previous:Null<Array<BenchResult>> and appends a
Change column inline when a baseline exists. Removes printComparison.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ChannelIterator: fast channel drain iterator

Replace the AsyncGenerator-based ChannelReaderTools.iterator() with a
dedicated ChannelIterator<T> that avoids per-item coroutine overhead.

The @:coroutine hasNext() method:
- Fast path: calls tryRead() first; when the buffer has items this
  succeeds immediately with no suspension at all
- Slow path: only calls waitForRead() when the buffer is empty,
  suspending once per "burst" of items rather than once per item
- Handles spurious wakeups with a while(true) retry loop

This restores pipeline/three_stage from ~2,000 to ~20,000 ops/sec on
eval (10x), matching the throughput of a manual waitForRead/tryRead
drain loop, while keeping the ergonomic `for (item in channel)` syntax.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add total time row to benchmark table; add build-lua.hxml

- printTable() now accumulates elapsedMs across all rows and prints a
  separator + "total" row at the bottom of the table (both plain and
  Change-column layouts align correctly because the separator is reused)
- benchmarks/build-lua.hxml added, mirroring tests/build-lua.hxml
- lua added to getTarget() so results are saved as latest-lua.json

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tune BenchConfig: split HL tier, scale up interpreter targets

All targets now run for roughly 3-8 s total (previously ranged from
110 ms to 26 s):

- HL split from cpp/jvm into its own tier (25k/3k/100k) — HL channel
  ops are ~30x slower than CPP/JVM so the combined tier caused 26 s runs
- JS scaled up 2.5x (500k/30k/1M) — old config finished in ~1.6 s
- python/php split and each scaled up ~50x/~25x respectively (old
  combined config finished in 110-230 ms)
- eval/neko/lua scaled up ~1.6x (13k/5k/80k) — old config was ~3.8 s
- cpp/jvm unchanged (~7.6 s / ~4.8 s)

Verified totals: eval 6.9 s, python 7.1 s, php 5.4 s, hl 4.4 s.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
Fix 15 signal quality bugs, late-entry issues, and stat calculation errors (#84)

* Initial plan

* Fix 15 bugs, optimizations and add 19 new tests per deep code review

BUG 1: Add vol_threshold param to detect_market_structure_shift()
  - CH1=0.70 (default), CH2=0.60, CH3=0.50 - fixes gate blocker killing 50%+ signals
BUG 2: Add MACD/BB/CVD/EMA ribbon scoring to run_confluence_check_relaxed()
  - CH2 can now reach HIGH confidence (+40 possible extra points)
BUG 3: Normalize confluence score display to /100 in format_message()
  - Raw score ÷ 150 × 100, capped at 100 - accurate display
BUG 4: Asymmetric entry zone (LONG: low=price−ATR×0.5, high=price+ATR×0.15)
  - SHORT: low=price−ATR×0.15, high=price+ATR×0.5 - biased toward discount/premium
BUG 5: Stale-close deferred when current price is within entry zone
  - Prevents confusing STALE alerts while subscribers hold active positions
BUG 6: BE trigger uses entry_low (LONG) or entry_high (SHORT) as reference
  - Not midpoint - more accurate BE trigger for actual fill prices
BUG 7: SL hit after BE triggered now records as 'BE' outcome, not 'LOSS'
  - Prevents win-rate inflation from counting TP1+BE trades as WIN+LOSS
BUG 8: ATR floor for trailing SL (min_trail_distance_atr_mult=1.0)
  - Prevents noise-driven stop-outs in volatile markets
TIMING 1: Reduce AUTO_SCAN_INTERVAL_SECONDS default from 60 → 30
  - Halves signal latency for the REST fallback scanner
TIMING 2: WATCHING pre-alert via on_near_miss callback in run_confluence_check()
  - Broadcasts preparation alert when 1/5 gates fail (near-miss)
OPT 1: Extract _ema_series() to module level
  - Eliminates 2 duplicate nested function definitions
OPT 2: Incremental RSI in detect_rsi_divergence() (O(n) vs O(n²))
  - Uses Wilder's smoothing - ~10x faster for 20-candle windows
OPT 4: Signal ID line added to format_message() broadcast
CALC 2: Sharpe Ratio uses daily bucketing + sqrt(365) annualisation
  - Correct for 24/7 crypto markets (not 252 trading days)
Tests: 19 new tests covering all fixes

commit.message:
fix(config): remove static sentry import + turbopack.root to restore standalone build

Root cause of missing /app/server.js in Docker:
1. Static 'import { withSentryConfig } from @sentry/nextjs' at the top
   of next.config.ts was crashing on load (serialize-javascript@7.0.3
   override broke a transitive dep). Next.js fell back to default config
   with no output: standalone.
2. turbopack.root: path.resolve(__dirname, '..') pointed to / in Docker
   (/app/..) which could trigger unexpected build behavior.

Fix:
- Remove static Sentry import; use dynamic import() inside the condition
  so a broken @sentry/nextjs never corrupts the config file load
- Remove turbopack.root (was only silencing a dev warning, not needed in prod)
- Restore cache-from in CI now that the real bug is fixed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

(express-only) instead of the full QoS. Mark existing QoS-accepting
overloads as @Deprecated with ReplaceWith guidance. Update tests to use
the new ReplyQoS-based API.

  control for reply messages (not "fixed by the protocol")
- Refactor JNIQuery.replySuccess to accept individual fields instead
  of Sample, avoiding QoS dependency in the non-deprecated code path
- Refactor JNIQuery.replyDelete to accept express:Boolean instead of QoS

* fix: remove deprecated priority/congestion_control calls in JNI reply

The protocol uses the request's priority and congestion control for replies.
The Rust Zenoh library deprecated these methods on the ReplyBuilder because
they have no effect. Remove the deprecated method calls and the unused
parameters to fix Clippy warnings with -D warnings.

* fix: fully remove priority/congestion_control params from JNI reply signatures

The previous commit stopped using these parameters but left them in the
function signatures with underscore prefixes. This removes them entirely
from both the Kotlin external fun declarations and Rust extern "C" fn
signatures, completing the JNI interface simplification.

but keep priority/congestionControl removed from JNI native calls.
Revert Query.kt to main version (QoS defaults, Sample construction).
Delete ReplyQoS.kt (not in Java PR).
Update QueryableTest.kt to use default QoS and assert individual
fields without priority/congestionControl expectations.

* feat: add ReplyQoS data class and update reply API

Create ReplyQoS data class with only `express` field, since the protocol
uses the request's priority and congestion control for reply messages.

Add new reply/replyDel overloads accepting ReplyQoS in Query.kt and
deprecate existing overloads that accept QoS. Update tests to use
ReplyQoS.

Resolves #616

Fix source generator diagnostics to support #pragma warning disable (#401)

* Fix source generator diagnostics to support #pragma warning disable

Split the RegisterSourceOutput pipeline in PolyTypeGenerator into two
separate pipelines: one for source generation (fully incremental) and
one for diagnostics (combined with CompilationProvider).

The diagnostic pipeline recovers the SyntaxTree from the Compilation
at emission time and uses Location.Create(SyntaxTree, TextSpan) to
produce SourceLocation instances instead of ExternalFileLocation
instances. Only SourceLocation is checked against pragma directives
by the Roslyn diagnostic filter.

Fixes the issue described in https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/92509
where diagnostics emitted from incremental source generators using
Location.Create(filePath, textSpan, linePositionSpan) create
ExternalFileLocation instances that bypass #pragma warning disable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Factor diagnostics out of TypeShapeProviderModel

Separate diagnostics from the incremental model so that positional
changes in diagnostic locations do not trigger expensive source code
regeneration. The parser now returns a (model, diagnostics) tuple,
and the generator uses Select to extract the model for source gen
(deduplicating via record equality) while diagnostics flow through
a separate pipeline.

Since diagnostics are no longer part of the model's equality chain,
EquatableDiagnostic is no longer needed as an intermediary. Raw
Diagnostic objects retain their original SourceLocation, which
natively supports #pragma warning disable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(mc): use actual neighbour surface_caches to eliminate chunk seams

The smooth MC density grid has an extra boundary column on each XZ axis
(xi=gx-1, zi=gz-1) to give edge cubes all 8 corners.  This column lies
one voxel outside the current chunk, so surface_cache has no entry for it.

Previous attempts (clamping = flat shelf, extrapolation = overshoot bumps)
both guessed the value.  The correct approach is to look up the exact
surface_y the neighbouring chunk will use for its own first column.

Changes:
- extract_chunk_mesh_smooth now accepts neighbor_x and neighbor_z
  (Option<&SurfaceCache> for the +X and +Z neighbours).
- Boundary grid points use neighbor.get(&(max_voxel.x, wz)) / etc.
  directly; if the neighbour is not yet loaded we fall back to clamping
  (minor flat shelf until it loads).
- All three call sites in metaworld_alpha.rs updated: neighbours are
  looked up with immutable borrows and cloned before the mutable borrow
  on the current chunk (dirty loop restructured from loaded_chunks_mut
  to a collect-then-per-ID pattern to allow this).

Bump: v0.1.70 → v0.1.71

* feat: inventory allocation system

* Add inventory sync endpoints, task APIs & UI

Introduce inventory snapshot/delta endpoints and related client sync logic, plus job task APIs and UI improvements. Key changes:

- New API routes: /api/inventory/items/snapshot and /api/inventory/items/delta to support initial snapshots and incremental deltas (with change threshold & caching).
- Allocations/transactions: allocations GET supports with_relations and limit; transactions GET supports limit and returns recent transactions with item names.
- Estimate endpoints updated to use the inventory allocation service: adding/updating/removing estimate items now integrates with allocations and returns allocation/stock info; InsufficientStockError handled with 409 responses.
- Job task APIs: added CRUD routes for tasks and a dedicated status endpoint with validation, rate limiting, and inventory side effects (reserve, consume, release). New task domain/repository files and a migration for job card tasks/inventory delta.
- Inventory page UI: show On Hand / Reserved / Available columns, reserved stock and recent activity cards, fetches allocations and transactions, and refresh behavior after adjustments.
- Misc: moved script imports to src paths, added jest.setup.d.ts, new hooks (use-inventory-snapshot, use-job-tasks), and many repository/use-case updates to support allocation flows.

This commit ties inventory reservation/consumption into estimates and job tasks, adds sync endpoints for efficient client caching, and surfaces reservations/activity in the tenant inventory UI.

* Add inventory sync endpoints, task APIs & UI

Introduce inventory snapshot/delta endpoints and related client sync logic, plus job task APIs and UI improvements. Key changes:

- New API routes: /api/inventory/items/snapshot and /api/inventory/items/delta to support initial snapshots and incremental deltas (with change threshold & caching).
- Allocations/transactions: allocations GET supports with_relations and limit; transactions GET supports limit and returns recent transactions with item names.
- Estimate endpoints updated to use the inventory allocation service: adding/updating/removing estimate items now integrates with allocations and returns allocation/stock info; InsufficientStockError handled with 409 responses.
- Job task APIs: added CRUD routes for tasks and a dedicated status endpoint with validation, rate limiting, and inventory side effects (reserve, consume, release). New task domain/repository files and a migration for job card tasks/inventory delta.
- Inventory page UI: show On Hand / Reserved / Available columns, reserved stock and recent activity cards, fetches allocations and transactions, and refresh behavior after adjustments.
- Misc: moved script imports to src paths, added jest.setup.d.ts, new hooks (use-inventory-snapshot, use-job-tasks), and many repository/use-case updates to support allocation flows.

This commit ties inventory reservation/consumption into estimates and job tasks, adds sync endpoints for efficient client caching, and surfaces reservations/activity in the tenant inventory UI.

commit.message:
Phase 3: Implement unified CLI interface with menu system and registry

## Overview
- Created etl_core/cli module for unified command-line interface
- Single entry point for all 5 ETL pipelines
- Interactive menu system with pipeline selection
- Configuration management for each pipeline
- Pipeline execution engine with progress reporting
- 29 integration tests (all passing)

## Components Implemented

1. **Menu System** (etl_core/cli/menu.py)
   - Interactive main menu with 5 pipelines + options
   - Pipeline-specific configuration input
   - Documentation and system status displays
   - Graceful error handling

2. **Pipeline Registry** (etl_core/cli/registry.py)
   - Central registry for all 5 pipelines
   - Pipeline metadata (name, description, status, version)
   - Configuration schema per pipeline
   - Active/COMING SOON status tracking
   - Dynamic pipeline class loading

3. **Execution Engine** (etl_core/cli/executor.py)
   - Instantiate and run pipelines with config
   - Capture execution metrics (duration, rows, errors)
   - Generate JSON execution reports
   - Error handling and recovery

4. **Progress Reporting** (etl_core/cli/progress.py)
   - Real-time progress bar display
   - Current/total row counts
   - Status messages and spinners
   - Summary reporting

5. **CLI Entry Point** (etl_core/cli/__main__.py)
   - Main entry point: python -m etl_core.cli
   - Menu-driven interactive interface
   - Graceful Ctrl+C handling

## Pipeline Status

- **B2ALL:** ACTIVE (migrated to Phase 2, working with CLI)
- **Amazon Manager:** COMING SOON
- **Price List (ap7):** COMING SOON
- **Catalog Merger (apgd):** COMING SOON
- **TIM Price List:** COMING SOON

## B2ALL Integration

- Updated B2ALLMergerPipeline to accept input_dir/output_dir
- Added execute() method for CLI executor compatibility
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing code
- Configuration can be passed during initialization

## Test Coverage

- 29 new integration tests for CLI components
- Tests cover: registry, menu, configuration, error handling
- All tests passing (100%)
- Total test suite: 226/226 passing

## Files Added/Modified

Added:
- etl_core/cli/__init__.py          (Main CLI module)
- etl_core/cli/menu.py              (Interactive menu)
- etl_core/cli/registry.py          (Pipeline registry)
- etl_core/cli/executor.py          (Execution engine)
- etl_core/cli/progress.py          (Progress reporting)
- etl_core/cli/__main__.py           (Entry point)
- tests/integration/test_cli_registry.py (29 tests)

Modified:
- etl_core/pipelines/b2all_merger/merger.py (Updated __init__, added execute())
- tests/unit/test_b2all_merger_migration.py (Updated for new API)

## Next Steps (Phase 3B)

1. Create placeholder pipeline modules for COMING SOON pipelines
2. Implement REST API (Flask) for remote execution
3. Add command-line argument support (--pipeline, --config)
4. Create web dashboard for monitoring
5. Implement workflow composition (pipeline chaining)

## Verification

- All 226 tests passing (197 unit + 29 integration)
- CLI registry functional
- Pipeline discovery working
- Configuration validation in place
- Execution engine ready for pipelines

commit.message:
Add detailed bug fixes documentation with root causes and lessons learned

## Overview
- Document all 7 bugs identified and fixed in Phase 1 & 2
- 100% fix rate (7/7 fixed)
- 197/197 tests passing

## Bug Documentation
Each bug includes:
- Component location with line numbers
- Detailed issue description
- Root cause analysis
- Solution code examples (before/after)
- Impact assessment
- Test cases and verification
- Lessons learned

## Bugs Documented

1. **Database Transaction Nesting (CRITICAL)**
   - Nested transactions auto-commit, breaking rollbacks
   - Solution: Remove nested transaction wrappers

2. **EAN Decimal Normalization (HIGH)**
   - Validation before cleanup caused failures
   - Solution: Normalize (remove decimal) before validation

3-4. **Weight Validator Range (HIGH)**
   - MAX_WEIGHT=1000 too restrictive for grams
   - Solution: Increased to 1,000,000 for flexible units

5. **XML Stream Parser Memory (CRITICAL)**
   - elem.clear() after yield destroyed consumer access
   - Solution: Remove clear(); let Python GC handle it

6. **Column Mapper Fuzzy Threshold (MEDIUM)**
   - Threshold 0.7 too high for cross-language matching
   - Solution: Lower to 0.3 for Polish-English support

7. **Excel Sheet Selection (MEDIUM)**
   - Reader defaulted to empty 'Sheet1' instead of 'Data'
   - Solution: Explicitly specify sheet_name parameter

## Prevention Strategies
- Test with realistic data early
- Test edge cases first (decimals, unicode, large values)
- Never nest context managers
- Validate API contracts thoroughly
- Document threshold and design choices
- Make required parameters explicit
- Order of operations matters (normalize before validate)

## Learning Outcomes
- Database transactions aren't nestable for same connection
- Generators shouldn't modify yielded objects
- Fuzzy matching is language-dependent
- Order of operations affects validation results
- API defaults can hide bugs
- Integration tests catch more bugs than unit tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
